analysis_full_md:
# CAVA Group, Inc. (CAVA) — Q1 FY2026 Earnings Review

> Full 8-task run. Sources: Press Release [SE-PR] · Transcript [SE-CT] · Investor Slides [SE-IS] · 10-Q [SE-QK] · 10-broker sell-side compilation [SE-SS] · Granite Drivers [SE-DR]. Fiscal Q1 ended Apr 19, 2026; reported May 19, 2026. Every quantitative line carries printed Street consensus + a named-broker cross-check.

---

## Executive Summary — The View

CAVA delivered a clean beat across every Street line — revenue +4.5%, EBITDA +8.7%, EPS and comps well ahead — into a soft, promotional QSR tape. The 9.7% comp was traffic-led (6.8% of it), so this is share gain through execution, not price or macro. Management raised FY26 comp (midpoint +150bps to 5.5%) and EBITDA guidance while barely touching margin guidance — a deliberately conservative posture multiple desks call beatable. The print de-risks the growth algorithm; valuation (~58x EBITDA) is now the debate.

**The three things that matter:** (1) traffic-led re-acceleration (9.7% vs Street 6.2%, ahead of the ~8% buy-side whisper); (2) the honeymoon-fade bear was dismantled (2024 class still positive at 18 months, productivity >100%, Blair TAM ~2,000 units); (3) the raise reads conservative (mid 5.5% vs a ~10% run-rate). **Risks:** ~58x valuation, flat RLM with H2 salmon/energy headwinds, an implied H2 deceleration, and unresolved salmon permanence.

---

## Task 1: P&L Extraction

### P&L Summary: CAVA Group, Inc. | Q1 2026 · GAAP P&L; Adj. EBITDA non-GAAP · 16-wk vs 16-wk PY

| Metric | $MMs | PY$ | y/y % | vs Consensus | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Revs** | 438 | 332 | 32% | $438 vs $419 — +4.5% beat | Traffic +6.8%, price/mix +2.9%; Blair +32% vs +26% cons |
| **RLP** | 109 | 82.3 | 32% | in-line | Restaurant-Level Profit +32.3%; COGS beat ~60bps (mix) |
| **RLM %** | 25.1% | 25.1% | 0 bps | 25.1% vs 25.0% — in-line, flat | Leverage offset by 3P-mix + wage; JPM 80bps below JPMe |
| **EPS** | 0.20 | 0.22 | (9%) | $0.20 vs $0.17 — beat | Beat despite y/y drop; tax optic (lower equity-comp benefit); PBT +48% |
| **G&A** | 51.6 | 41.4 | 25% | in-line (JPM) | 11.8% of rev vs 12.5% (-70bps); ex-SBC 9.9% vs 10.5% |
| **OP** | 25.3 | 15.7 | 61% | — | RLP growth + G&A leverage; pre-opening 6.2 vs 4.5 |
| **OM %** | 5.8% | 4.7% | +110 bps | — | Operating leverage on outsized SRS |
| **Adj EBITDA** | 61.7 | 44.9 | 38% | $61.7 vs $57.3 — +8.7% beat | RBC: +49bps margin ahead; Blair +38% vs +28% cons |
| **EM %** | 14.1% | 13.5% | +57 bps | ahead | Top-line upside flowed through |

*Read:* clean beat on every line vs Street; the only non-beat is RLM (in-line/flat) — deliberate (3P-mix + wage), not a miss. EPS-down y/y is a tax-line optic (PBT +48%).

---

## Task 2: Guidance Analysis (FY2026, raised)

**Macro posture:** Conservative / hedged. SSS midpoint raised +150bps to 5.5% (range 4.5–6.5%) yet Q1 printed 9.7% and Q2-to-date tracks ~9.7% — the guide embeds an H2 deceleration management isn't yet seeing. RLM guide raised only +10bps at the top despite the comp raise (salmon ~100bps + 2% wage + 20-40bps energy offset the leverage). Pre-opening raised $19.5–20M → $22–22.5M (pipeline timing).

**Common narrative:** *Why:* execution-driven beat + NRO pipeline tracking >100% productivity. *Trends:* SSS re-accelerated to ~10% (2-yr traffic 7.2%) while QSR peers decelerate/discount. *Noise:* ignore "EPS miss" (tax optic) and "RLM flat" alarm (deliberate reinvestment).

| Metric | Guidance (May 19) | Consensus | Prior (Feb 24) | Mgmt Explanation | Sell-side Cross-check |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Net New Units** | 75–77 | 76 | 74–76 | Accelerated pipeline; NRO productivity >100% | RBC: 20 in Q1 beat Street 16 |
| **SSS %** | 4.5–6.5% | 5.1% | 3.0–5.0% | Q1 traffic + menu; H2 moderate MSD | RBC: mid +150bps to 5.5%, ahead of Street |
| **RLM %** | 23.7–24.3% | 24.0% | 23.7–24.2% | +10bps top only; -100bps salmon, -20-40bps energy, +wage | Stifel: leverage almost entirely offset |
| **Pre-opening** | $22.0–22.5M | $19.8M | $19.5–20.0M | Pipeline timing, higher site entries | RBC: +12.7% mid, supports units |
| **Adj EBITDA** | $181–191M | $186–187M | $176–184M | +$6M mid on Q1 flow-through | Stifel: encompasses cons $187M |

---

## Task 3: Segment / Channel Summary

| Segment | Revs | Margin Y/Y | Notes | Sell-Side |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **CAVA (brand)** | 434 vs 328 +32% | RLM 25.1% flat | 459 units (+20.2%); AUV $3.0M (vs $2.3M 1st-yr target); FB&P 29.1% (-20bps), Labor 25.7% (flat), Occupancy 6.9% (-50bps), Other OpEx 13.3% (+80bps, 3P) | RBC/Blair: RLM 25.1% vs 25.0% cons |
| **Other** | 3.9 vs 3.3 +16% | — | CAVA Foods mfg + non-restaurant | RBC: immaterial to multiple |
| **Digital (channel)** | ~40% mix | profit-neutral | Mix 39.9% vs ~36% PY; digital rev +1,080bps q/q (1P+3P); KDS investment | Priced dollar-profit-neutral (optic) |

---

## Task 4: KPI Summary

| Metric | Value | Y/Y | Mgmt Explanation | Sell-side Cross-check |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **% CAVA SSS** | 9.7% | (110) bps | Lapped +10.8% PY; traffic-led; 2-yr stack double-digit 3rd straight Q | RBC: beat Street 6.2% + buy-side ~8% whisper |
| **% Traffic** | 6.8% | — | Below-CPI pricing drives visits | JPM: 6.8% vs 6.0%e — the surprise |
| **% Price/Mix** | 2.9% | — | ~1.4% Jan price (base bowl/pita flat) + mix | RBC: only 1.4% price → traffic quality |
| **CAVA Units** | 459 | +20% | 21 opens, 1 closure; 29 states + DC | RBC: 459 beat cons 455 |
| **Net New Openings** | 20 | +33% | 16-wk Q1; Midwest entries; >100% productivity | RBC: 20 beat Street 16 → 4.9% rev beat |
| **AUV** | $3,027 | +3% | System-wide $3.0M; new units >$3M vs $2.3M target | RBC/Blair: best-in-class unit econ |
| **$ RLM** | 25.1% | 0 bps | Leverage offset by 3P-mix + wage | RBC: in-line w/ cons; flat = labor reinvest |
| **Digital Mix** | 39.9% | +~390 bps | 3P + 1P up; KDS investment | Blair: digital rev +1,080bps q/q |

---

## Task 5: Earnings Call — Management Discussion & Q&A

**Management discussion (prepared remarks):**
- Headline: Revs +32% $438M; NI $23.6M vs $25.7M (tax + D&A); Adj EBITDA +38% $61.7M.
- SSS quality: 9.7% on 6.8% traffic; *"a clear industry leader… meet the moment for the modern consumer."*
- Footprint: 20 NROs → 459 (+20.2%); Midwest (Cincinnati/St. Louis/Columbus); 2026 cohort ≥2025; honeymoon NOT fading (2024 class + at 18mo).
- Salmon: national launch (first seafood; ~100bps RLM headwind, "penny-profit neutral"); White Sweet Potato drove frequency + COGS leverage; shrimp in Nashville market test.
- Data/AI: CAVA Core + CAVA Current live ("decade of data transformation"); predictive prep/labor ahead. AGM role >50% of higher-volume stores.
- Liquidity: zero debt, $403M cash, $150M undrawn revolver; OCF $64.1M, FCF $15.5M (vs $2.7M PY).

**Q&A — synthesized themes:**
- **SSS sustainability** *(analyst):* Q2 ~Q1 (~9.7%), above guide; buffer for tough comps + macro.
- **Margin mechanics** *(analyst):* RLM flat = 3P-mix (profit-neutral) + 2% wage + H2 energy; Other OpEx +80bps; won't take price.
- **Honeymoon** *(BofA/Baird):* 2024 class outpacing at 18mo — the fade-bear refuted.
- **SSS breadth** *(TD Cowen):* all regions/vintages/income; lower-income outperforming (k-shaped bridge); no Iran-conflict hit.
- **Salmon permanence** *(Morgan Stanley):* runs ≥4Q, NOT committed everyday — stays open.
- **Pricing power** *(Citi):* would NOT take price; AB1228 precedent — chose not to price, traffic outperformed.
- **Catering / capital** *(JPM):* Houston test → 2nd market; $400M cash, highest use = NRO (gated by leader pipeline).

---

## Task 6: Sell-Side Summary (10 brokers, May 18–20; stock +~7% AH)

**Ratings/PTs:** Piper OW PT→$105 (raised) · JPM OW $90 · Blair/Baird/Roth/RBC/Jefferies constructive (raising ests) · Loop HOLD $85 · Davidson NEUTRAL $84 (↑$80) · Stifel cautious. **Consensus-beat scoreboard:** SSS 9.7% vs 6.0% · Revs $438 vs $419 · RLM 25.1% vs 25.0% · EBITDA $61.7 vs $57.3 · EPS $0.20 vs $0.17.

**Five bullish:** (1) 9.7% comp beat Street 6.2% AND ~8% buy-side whisper; (2) NRO productivity >100%, Blair TAM ~2,000 units; (3) low-income cohort outperforming (k-shaped); (4) EBITDA beat 8.7%, +49bps margin ahead; (5) pristine balance sheet (zero debt, $400M cash).

**Five bearish:** (1) ~58x FY26 EBITDA — Davidson PT $84 assumes contraction to 43x FY27 (the dominant bear); (2) RLM flat/in-line, not expanding; (3) guide implies sharp H2 deceleration; (4) salmon permanence/margin unresolved; (5) EPS down y/y (tax optic) + rising pre-opening/D&A.

**Macro/ecosystem:** QSR soft/promotional — CAVA gaining share without discounting; energy/fuel-surcharge + polyethylene a new H2 input watch; Midwest validates cross-country Mediterranean scale vs burger/burrito incumbents.

---

## Task 7: Stock Reaction (+~7% after-hours)

1. **Traffic-led comp crushed the bar** (9.7% vs Street 6.2%, ~350bps; vs ~8% buy-side). Elite traffic insulation — CAVA the share consolidator (trade-down from casual dining, trade-up from QSR). The prime mover.
2. **The honeymoon-fade bear was dismantled** — de-risks the growth algorithm, which matters to the multiple more than the print.
3. **Conservative raise** (mid 5.5% vs ~10% run-rate) sets an easily-beatable bar → re-raise optionality.

**Offset (why +7%, not euphoric):** ~58x EBITDA caps upside (bull case largely in the stock); flat RLM + EPS-down optics + implied H2 decel gave cautious desks (Loop HOLD, Davidson/Stifel NEUTRAL) cover. *Strong, but priced for greatness.*

---

## Task 8: Drivers Analysis (the loop)

> Run AGAINST CAVA's saved Granite Drivers [SE-DR], with evidence cross-read from the quarter's book. Per-debate "what happened this quarter."

### Section 1 — Per-Debate Progress

| Saved Debate | What happened this quarter | Evidence (number + source) | Verdict |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **POS a) RLM cushion from reinvesting; easy comps rest of 2026** | RLM held flat, not expanded, despite 9.7% comp — cushion being reinvested, not banked. Easy-comp thesis intact. | RLM 25.1% flat [SE-PR]; guide +10bps top only [SE-PR]; "leverage almost entirely offset by cost" [SE-SS Stifel] | **Live — reinvested, not banked** |
| **POS b) AGM and other investments made** | AGM rollout working — covered restaurants outperforming uncovered; scaling. | "AGM coverage… outperforming those without" [SE-CT]; ">50% of higher-volume locations" [SE-CT] | **Resolving Bull** |
| **POS c) Shrimp as next item; pipeline?** | Shrimp → market test (Nashville), last stage before national. Salmon launched as tent-pole. | Shrimp "moved to market test" [SE-CT]; salmon national, ~100bps headwind [SE-CT] | **Progressing** |
| **POS d) Catering?** | Early/gated — Houston test → 2nd market; not yet scaled, no $ contribution. | "expand to a second market test… load balancing" [SE-CT]; 20+ purpose-built kitchens | **Live — early, no $ yet** |
| **NEG a) Lap comps in Q1-2027? Need more menu items?** | Unresolved (FY27 question) but de-risked — salmon ≥4Q, shrimp in test, loyalty/digital flywheel building. | 2-yr traffic stack 7.2% (up seq from 7.1%) [SE-SS Blair]; salmon ≥4Q [SE-CT] | **Live — pipeline forming** |
| **NEG b) Keep RLM flat next year again?** | Effectively YES for FY26 — guide implies flat-ish RLM (23.7–24.3%) as salmon/wage/energy absorb leverage. Bear confirmed by choice. | RLM guide +10bps top only [SE-PR]; salmon ~100bps + energy 20-40bps + wage [SE-SS] | **Resolving Bear (flat deliberately)** |
| **NEG c) New-store honeymoon + tough-comp recur?** | Directly refuted — 2024 class positive at 18mo, no cliff; productivity >100%. Biggest structural overhang weakened. | "2024 class… outpacing at 18mo" [SE-CT]; AUV $3.0M vs $2.3M target; Blair TAM ~2,000 units [SE-SS] | **Resolving Bull (bear weakened)** |

### Section 2 — KPI Scorecard (vs tracked KPIs)
% SSS 9.7% (-110bps, traffic-led beat) · % Traffic 6.8% (the surprise) · % Price 2.9% (~1.4% list) · AUV $3,027k (+3%) · RLM 25.1% (flat — central debate) · Food 29.1% (-20bps) · Labor 25.7% (flat) · Occupancy 6.9% (-50bps) · Units 459 (+20%) · Openings 20 (+33%) · Menu: salmon (national), shrimp (test).

### Section 3 — Triggers Check
- **a) Watch for strong menu launch item → FIRED.** Glazed salmon (first-ever seafood, national, in line w/ tests) + White Sweet Potato return.
- **b) Watch for sell-side upgrade on good data → FIRED.** Piper PT→$105; Blair/Baird/Roth raising ests; Davidson $80→$84.

### Section 4 — New & Retiring Debates
**New (track these):** (1) Energy/fuel-surcharge margin drag, H2-weighted (20-40bps) — the swing factor on FY26 RLM. (2) Digital-mix → margin optics (~40%, Other-OpEx +80bps). (3) Data/AI platform (CAVA Core + Current) — long-dated lever + execution risk.
**Retire/downgrade:** NEG c) honeymoon recurrence — two quarters of evidence answered it; downgrade to a quarterly 2-yr-stack watch.

### Section 5 — Updated View & Quarter Recap
**Current point of view:** bull intact and strengthening on the top line (traffic, NRO productivity, honeymoon-bear weakened, ~2,000-unit TAM); bear has migrated from growth to **margin + valuation** (RLM flat by choice; ~58x prices the bull in). High conviction on durability, gated on multiple.

**Append to Quarter recaps:**
> **Q1-26: Blowout, traffic-led.** 9.7% comp (6.8% traffic) beat Street 6.2%; Revs +4.5% / EBITDA +8.7% vs cons; RLM flat 25.1% (deliberate reinvestment). Guide raised (SSS mid +150bps to 5.5%) but conservative. Honeymoon-fade bear weakened; salmon national, shrimp in test. Margin + ~58x valuation now the bear. Stock +7% AH.

task8_md:
# Task 8 — Drivers Analysis: CAVA Q1 FY2026

> The loop (DOMAIN_MODEL §2) run AGAINST CAVA's saved Granite Drivers [SE-DR], with evidence cross-read from the quarter's book (PR / transcript / slides / 10-Q / 10-broker sell-side). This is the per-debate "what happened this quarter" — the product piece. Produced from the engine-assembled prompt (`analysis.run`, CAVA Q1-2026; [SE-DR] + 6 source keys in context).

---

## Section 1 — Per-Debate Progress (the core)

| Saved Debate | What happened this quarter | Evidence (number + source) | Verdict |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **POS a) RLM cushion from reinvesting; easy comps rest of 2026** | RLM held flat, NOT expanded, despite a 9.7% comp — the "cushion" is being spent on reinvestment, not banked. Easy-comp thesis intact (lapped +10.8%, still printed 9.7%). | RLM 25.1% flat y/y [SE-PR]; guide raised only +10bps at top to 23.7–24.3% [SE-PR]; "leverage almost entirely offset by cost" [SE-SS Stifel] | **Live — cushion being reinvested, not banked** |
| **POS b) AGM and other investments made** | AGM rollout is working — covered restaurants outperforming uncovered; penetration scaling. | "restaurants with AGM coverage are outperforming those without" [SE-CT]; ">50% of (higher-volume) locations" [SE-CT]; RBC/JPM cite as upside lever [SE-SS] | **Resolving Bull** |
| **POS c) Shrimp as next item; what else in pipeline?** | Roasted garlic shrimp advanced to market test (Nashville) — last stage before national. Salmon launched nationally as the tent-pole. Pipeline visible + disciplined. | Shrimp "moved to market test" [SE-CT]; salmon national launch, ~100bps RLM headwind, "penny-profit neutral" [SE-CT] | **Progressing** |
| **POS d) Catering?** | Still gated/early — Houston test expanding to a 2nd market; explicitly not yet scaled; demand acknowledged "tremendous." An unquantified future lever, not a this-quarter contributor. | "expand to a second market test later this year… load balancing" [SE-CT]; 20+ purpose-built hybrid/digital kitchens [SE-CT] | **Live — early, no $ yet** |
| **NEG a) How lap comps in Q1-2027? Need more menu items?** | Not resolved (it's a FY27 question) but de-risked at the margin: salmon runs ≥4Q, shrimp in test, loyalty/digital flywheel building — a menu pipeline is forming to lap this year's strength. | Salmon ≥4Q [SE-CT]; shrimp market test [SE-CT]; 2-yr traffic stack 7.2% (up seq from 7.1%) [SE-SS Blair] | **Live — pipeline forming, unresolved** |
| **NEG b) Keep RLM flat next year again?** | Effectively YES for FY26 — guide implies flat-ish RLM (23.7–24.3% vs 25.1% Q1) as salmon (~100bps), 2% wage, and 20–40bps energy absorb the sales leverage. The bear is being confirmed, by choice. | RLM guide +10bps top only [SE-PR]; salmon ~100bps + energy 20–40bps + wage cited [SE-SS Stifel]; "won't take price" [SE-CT Citi] | **Resolving Bear (margin held flat deliberately)** |
| **NEG c) New-store honeymoon + tough-comp issue recur?** | Directly refuted this quarter — 2024 class still positive at 18 months, no honeymoon cliff; NRO productivity >100% across all geos/formats. The biggest structural overhang weakened. | "2024 class… outpacing expectations at 18mo" [SE-CT]; NRO productivity >100%, AUV $3.0M vs $2.3M 1st-yr target [SE-CT/SE-SS]; Blair TAM ~2,000 units [SE-SS] | **Resolving Bull (bear weakened)** |

---

## Section 2 — KPI Scorecard (vs the tracked KPIs in [SE-DR])

| KPI (tracked) | Value | Y/Y | Read |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| % SSS | 9.7% | (110) bps | Beat Street 6.2% [SE-SS]; traffic-led = high quality |
| % Traffic | 6.8% | — | The surprise; vs 6.0%e JPM [SE-SS] |
| % Price | 2.9% | — | Only ~1.4% Jan price → traffic-driven, not price |
| AUVs | $3,027k | +3% | New units >$3M vs $2.3M 1st-yr target |
| RLMs | 25.1% | 0 bps | Flat — the central margin debate |
| Food cost % rev | 29.1% | (20) bps | Favorable mix; salmon to pressure H2 |
| Labor % rev | 25.7% | flat | 2% wage offset by leverage |
| Occupancy % rev | 6.9% | (50) bps | Sales leverage |
| Unit growth | 459 | +20.2% | 29 states + DC; Midwest entry |
| New store openings | 20 | +33% | vs Street 16 [SE-SS] |
| Key menu items | Salmon (national), White Sweet Potato (returned), Shrimp (test) | — | Tent-pole + seasonal cadence |

---

## Section 3 — Triggers Check

| Trigger ([SE-DR]) | Status | Evidence |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **a) Watch for strong menu launch item** | **FIRED** | Glazed salmon — first-ever seafood, national launch, "in line with market tests," driving dinner daypart [SE-CT]. White Sweet Potato return drove frequency + COGS leverage. |
| **b) Watch for sell-side upgrade on good data** | **FIRED** | Piper PT→$105 (raised); Blair/Baird/Roth raising estimates; Davidson PT $80→$84 [SE-SS]. Constructive across the desk on the print. |

---

## Section 4 — New & Retiring Debates

**New debate this quarter (Granite not yet tracking — should):**
1. **Energy / fuel-surcharge margin drag (H2-weighted).** Newly explicit 20–40bps RLM headwind from fuel surcharges + polyethylene, Q3/Q4-loaded. Not in the current driver; it's the swing factor on the FY26 margin guide. *Why it matters: it's the difference between the low and high end of the RLM range.* [SE-CT/SE-SS]
2. **Digital mix → margin optics (~40%).** Digital hit 39.9% (3P up), priced dollar-profit-neutral but optically deleveraging the Other-OpEx line (+80bps). A recurring "is mix hurting margin?" question worth formalizing. [SE-CT/SE-SS]
3. **Data/AI platform (CAVA Core + Current).** Management flagged a "decade of data transformation" — predictive prep/labor/inventory. Long-dated, but a new structural lever (and a new execution risk) not in the driver. [SE-CT]

**Retire / downgrade:** *NEG c) new-store honeymoon recurrence* — two consecutive quarters of evidence (2024 class + at 18mo) have largely answered it; downgrade from active bear to a quarterly watch-item (the 2-yr stack).

---

## Section 5 — Updated View & Quarter Recap Line

**Current point of view (after Q1-2026):**
* **Bull intact and strengthening on the top line** — traffic-led 9.7% comp, NRO productivity >100%, honeymoon-fade bear weakened, ~2,000-unit TAM (Blair). The growth algorithm is de-risked.
* **Bear has migrated from growth to margin + valuation** — RLM is being held flat *by choice* (reinvestment + salmon/energy/wage), and at ~58x EBITDA the stock prices the bull case in. The debate is no longer "can they grow?" but "will margin ever inflect, and is it already paid for?"
* **Net:** high-conviction on durability, gated on multiple. Watch the FY27-lap setup and the H2 energy headwind.

**Append to [SE-DR] Quarter recaps:**
> **Q1-26: Blowout, traffic-led.** 9.7% comp (6.8% traffic) beat Street 6.2%; Revs +4.5% / EBITDA +8.7% vs cons; RLM flat 25.1% (deliberate reinvestment). Guide raised (SSS mid +150bps to 5.5%) but reads conservative. Honeymoon-fade bear weakened (2024 class + at 18mo); salmon national, shrimp in test. Margin + ~58x valuation now the bear. Stock +7% AH.
